events.title: It’s My Park at Bushwick Inlet Park
events.description: This It’s My Park season, volunteer with Friends of Bushwick Inlet Park to beautify Bushwick Inlet Park. We look forward to cleaning up the park with you! Volunteers should meet at 50 Kent Ave and N11th Streets. For more information, please email volunteer@bushwickinletpark.org.

events.description: The Journey is a sculptural installation by Brooklyn-based artist Risha Gorig that takes the shape of a flock of pink birds soaring in the sky. events.title: Wednesday Greenmarket at Bartel-Pritchard Square
events.description: Nestled inside Prospect Park’s tree-shaded southwest corner, this much-loved market is where South Slope and Windsor Terrace residents stock up on locally grown staples. The offerings range from a selection of vegetables, fruits, baked goods, plants and flowers to fresh-caught fish and organic baked goods. For a full calendar of events and a list of sellers,

events.title: Farm Community Volunteer Days
events.description: Help prepare the QBG Farm to grow produce that we distribute to food pantries to support our communities with food relief. Join QBG’s Farm staff for an engaging, educational event where you will learn gardening basics and work hands-on with plants. Make new friends and put your green thumb to good use in a variety of seasonal projects. Learn more and sign up

events.title: Free Summer Meals in Prospect Park
events.description: Enjoy free meals for youth ages 18 and under in Prospect Park this summer! Every day from June 29 through September 4, free meals will be distributed by the NYC Department of Education in partnership with Prospect Park Alliance at the Parkside and Ocean Avenue Entrance to Prospect Park. Visit the NYC DOE meals truck from 10 am–4 pm on weekdays and 10 am–3 pm

events.title: Prospect Park Stroller Walk
events.description: Calling all parents and caregivers with children! Join a Prospect Park Alliance Naturalist for a leisurely stroll along Prospect Park’s waterways. Embrace the sound of streams and songs of birds, while enjoying fragrant flowers and the towering trees of Brooklyn’s Backyard. Please note that this walk leaves promptly at 10 am from the Prospect Park Boathouse.

events.title: Intrepid Museum America's 250th Festival 2026: July 3 Free Family Day, NYC
events.description: Kick off July 4th weekend 2026 at the Intrepid Museum's free 'Celebrate America's 250th on the Hudson' festival on Pier 86 in Manhattan. Families can climb aboard visiting tall ships and enjoy live performances, interactive exhibits, and military demonstrations along the Hudson River. Free, family-friendly fun to start the Independence Day holiday.

events.description: Wrap up Fourth of July weekend 2026 at the Intrepid Museum's free 'Celebrate America's 250th on the Hudson' festival on Pier 86 in Manhattan. Families can board historic tall ships and take in live performances, interactive exhibits, and military demonstrations on the Hudson River. A free, all-ages way to cap off the holiday.

events.title: Smorgasburg
events.description: , presented in partnership with Prospect Park Alliance, is considered the “Woodstock of Eating.” The celebrated open-air food fair is back in Prospect Park for the 2026 season! Over 60 local businesses will gather on Breeze Hill, near the Lincoln Road entrance, to offer a range of food and drink every Sunday from April 5–October 25.

events.title: Exhibit: Battle Pass in Prospect Park
events.description: Join Prospect Park Alliance to learn more about the Revolutionary War’s Battle of Brooklyn, the first and largest battle of the American War for Independence, on its 250th anniversary with this replica of the famous Battle Pass. events.title: Exhibit: Black People and the Battle of Brooklyn
events.description: Join Prospect Park Alliance to learn about the important and varied roles Black people played in the Revolutionary War’s Battle of Brooklyn in August 1776, the first and largest battle of the American War for Independence. events.title: Exhibit: The Shirley Chisholm Way: Brooklyn’s Blueprint for Change
events.description: Join Prospect Park Alliance and Shirley Chisholm Cultural Institute (SCCI) for a new exhibit that explores how Brooklyn’s beloved daughter broke down gender and racial barriers to fight for her community, becoming the first Black woman elected to Congress.
